texto: The finding that rereading textbooks is often labor in vain ought to send a chill up the spines of educators and learners, because it’s the number one study strategy of most people—including more than 80 percent of college students in some surveys—and is central in what we tell ourselves to do during the hours we dedicate to learning. Rereading has three strikes against it. It is time consuming. It doesn’t result in durable memory. And it often involves a kind of unwitting self-deception, as growing familiarity with the text comes to feel like mastery of the content.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Peter C. Brown: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “The finding that rereading textbooks is often labor in vain ought to send a chill up the spines o…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que educacao, learning, reflexao pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de estudos e termino.
